title: The Red Fire Dispatch
description: A paper tape from the sounder, still in the receiving tray at the Sandusky instrument room. The message is marked with the double-dash prefix that designates red fire priority. It contains a physical description of three people — a tall man with a scar above his left eye, a woman with a child of approximately seven years — and names Aldous Greer of Columbus as the filing party. The description matches Elias Cutter's current passengers precisely. The filing timestamp shows the message was sent from Columbus at 9:47 PM, but Columbus is 140 miles south — Greer could not have filed it from Columbus if he is already within twelve miles of Sandusky.
